title: lobot-tui: The Terminal That Notices Everything
theme: A calm, technical control-room story about keeping a JupyterHub cluster upright while the web UI is hypothetically having a nap
style_anchor: btop-style terminal dashboard with crisp neon metrics, compact panes, and dry operational humor
accent: keyboard-driven admin operations with disaster-recovery composure

protagonist.description: A focused cluster operator in a dark hoodie, slightly tired eyes, badge clipped to the pocket, one hand on a mechanical keyboard and the other on a coffee that has clearly gone cold
protagonist.silhouette: slim figure, forward-leaning posture, hoodie hood down, rectangular monitor glow across the face

scenes.prompt:
Human-Readable house style: a mostly monochrome, hand-drawn editorial webcomic. The recurring protagonist is a minimalist stick figure with a round head, dot eyes, and a consistent silhouette. Backgrounds are detailed and grounded; characters remain deliberately simple. Use clean black ink, restrained hatching, and at most one warm orange accent color. Dialogue is short, legible, and hand-lettered in plain speech bubbles. The humor is dry, observational, technically aware, and never explained.

btop-style terminal dashboard with crisp neon metrics, compact panes, and dry operational humor

Render one self-contained cinematic comic scene, not a multi-panel page. Camera: wide.

Location: cluster operations room. Scene: A dim control room shows the Lobot cluster dashboard glowing on a large terminal while the operator stands center-frame, taking in pods, nodes, and resource groups at a glance. The interface looks calm in the way only something barely holding together can look calm.

Recurring protagonist (operator_01): A focused cluster operator in a dark hoodie, slightly tired eyes, badge clipped to the pocket, one hand on a mechanical keyboard and the other on a coffee that has clearly gone cold Silhouette: slim figure, forward-leaning posture, hoodie hood down, rectangular monitor glow across the face

Keep the protagonist's proportions, face, and silhouette identical across every scene. Accent color: keyboard-driven admin operations with disaster-recovery composure.

Visible prop continuity:
- main_terminal_01: Terminal dashboard; A wide terminal split into pods, nodes, and resource panes, with btop-like colors and tiny status bars that are trying their best.; Use as the primary dashboard display whenever the cluster view returns.
- cold_mug_01: Cold mug; A ceramic mug with a faint ring on the rim and the sort of coffee that has already become a memory.; First of several coffee mugs; do not reuse unless the exact mug returns.

World prop reference (IDs are metadata; never print an ID in the artwork):
main_terminal_01: Terminal dashboard; A wide terminal split into pods, nodes, and resource panes, with btop-like colors and tiny status bars that are trying their best.; continuity: Use as the primary dashboard display whenever the cluster view returns.
cold_mug_01: Cold mug; A ceramic mug with a faint ring on the rim and the sort of coffee that has already become a memory.; continuity: First of several coffee mugs; do not reuse unless the exact mug returns.
rg_table_display_01: Resource group table; A compact allocation table updated every five seconds, filtered to jupyter-* workloads, with enough detail to make silence feel earned.; continuity: Shows per-resource-group utilisation; can return in later scenes as the same table view.
wireless_keyboard_01: Mechanical keyboard; A compact mechanical keyboard with clearly worn WASD keys and a satisfying, low-pitched clack.; continuity: Primary input device for all keyboard-driven actions; keep keycaps and wear consistent.
node_table_display_01: Node allocation table; A dense node matrix with cordon and schedulable indicators, plus expandable disk sub-rows that look like they were designed by someone who respects consequences.; continuity: Use for node-level resource and cordon views; expandable disk rows remain associated with this table.
status_sticky_01: Status sticky note; A yellow sticky note with a hand-written reminder: ‘double-keypress confirms the irreversible stuff.’; continuity: Can appear near the keyboard as a reminder prop; do not reuse unless the same note returns.
log_stream_panel_01: Pod log stream; A focused log pane with colored timestamps, namespace tags, and a vertical stream that never quite stops being dramatic.; continuity: Use for log streaming and exec-related views; keep the same pane aesthetic if it returns.
shell_prompt_01: Exec shell prompt; A minimal shell prompt inside the pod, ready for bash, inspection, and the kind of debugging that sounds simpler than it is.; continuity: Represents exec bash into a pod; return only when the same pod shell is shown again.
drain_confirm_dialog_01: Drain confirmation dialog; A terse confirmation box that demands a second keypress before anything starts getting moved around.; continuity: Use for destructive node operations requiring double confirmation; keep wording consistent if reused.
tea_cup_01: Tea cup; A small white tea cup with an imperfect saucer and no visible tea, because the tea is clearly elsewhere now.; continuity: A new cup distinct from the earlier mug; do not reuse unless this exact cup returns.
whiteboard_plan_01: Runbook whiteboard; A marker-sketched plan with arrows, boxes, and a note that says ‘verify image tag before optimism.’; continuity: Can represent admin operations planning and rollout steps; maintain same markings if revisited.
dry_erase_marker_01: Dry-erase marker; A black marker with a chewed cap, resting diagonally like it has seen a few postmortems.; continuity: Keep cap state and marker color stable if the same marker returns.
headset_01: Operator headset; A lightweight over-ear headset hanging from one shoulder, with one earcup slightly scuffed from repeated late-night calls.; continuity: Recurring operator accessory; keep scuff mark consistent if it returns.
server_rack_window_01: Rack observation window; A narrow glass panel looking into the server room, with rows of LEDs reflecting like city traffic at midnight.; continuity: Use as an external glimpse of the same cluster environment; do not replace the view if it returns.
lanyard_badge_01: Admin badge; A clipped badge on a dark lanyard, swaying gently as the operator walks, with the logo worn slightly smooth.; continuity: Operator identity prop; stable if seen again.
desk_lamp_01: Desk lamp; A warm desk lamp aimed just enough to keep the keyboard visible without pretending this is comfortable.; continuity: New background object for the ending; do not reuse unless the exact lamp returns.

Dialogue to reproduce exactly and legibly:
- “Everything is green, which is either excellent or a very specific kind of warning.”

Use detailed environmental storytelling and readable prop labels. Do not add a title, scene number, caption explaining the joke, or metadata IDs.
scenes.description: A dim control room shows the Lobot cluster dashboard glowing on a large terminal while the operator stands center-frame, taking in pods, nodes, and resource groups at a glance. The interface looks calm in the way only something barely holding together can look calm.
dialogue: “Everything is green, which is either excellent or a very specific kind of warning.”

props.label: Terminal dashboard
props.description: A wide terminal split into pods, nodes, and resource panes, with btop-like colors and tiny status bars that are trying their best.
props.continuity: Use as the primary dashboard display whenever the cluster view returns.
props.interaction_detail: The pod table shows image tags, age, phase, and a suspiciously neat memory column updated in real time.

props.label: Cold mug
props.description: A ceramic mug with a faint ring on the rim and the sort of coffee that has already become a memory.
props.continuity: First of several coffee mugs; do not reuse unless the exact mug returns.
props.interaction_detail: The surface has a tiny crack that catches the monitor glow like a badge of honor.

- “This is the part where the interface checks whether I really meant it. I did, unfortunately.”

Use detailed environmental storytelling and readable prop labels. Do not add a title, scene number, caption explaining the joke, or metadata IDs.
scenes.description: The operator initiates a node drain and pauses at the double-keypress confirmation. The UI insists on certainty; the operator, after a beat, grants it. Somewhere deep in the cluster, a workload receives its notice politely and without drama.
dialogue: “This is the part where the interface checks whether I really meant it. I did, unfortunately.”

props.label: Drain confirmation dialog
props.description: A terse confirmation box that demands a second keypress before anything starts getting moved around.
props.continuity: Use for destructive node operations requiring double confirmation; keep wording consistent if reused.
props.interaction_detail: The dialog highlights the second prompt only after the first one is accepted, as if trying to be noble about it.

props.label: Tea cup
props.description: A small white tea cup with an imperfect saucer and no visible tea, because the tea is clearly elsewhere now.
props.continuity: A new cup distinct from the earlier mug; do not reuse unless this exact cup returns.
props.interaction_detail: There is a tiny tea stain on the saucer shaped vaguely like a cloud with priorities.
